# Appendix A: Environment Setup {#sec-appendix-a .unnumbered}

This appendix covers everything you need before Chapter 1: installing
dependencies, rendering the book, and — for Chapters 6–12 — cloning the
companion DDR Intelligence Platform repository.

## 1. Prerequisites

- Python 3.11 or later
- [Quarto](https://quarto.org) 1.7 or later (`quarto --version` to check)
- Git
- [Positron](https://positron.posit.co) (recommended) or any editor with
  a Python and Quarto extension

## 2. Set up the book's Python environment

From the `book/` directory:

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate          # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Or, if you prefer conda/mamba:

```bash
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate ddr-rag-book
```

## 3. Generate the sample dataset

Chapters 1–5 use a small synthetic DDR archive generated on demand (never
committed as PDFs, to keep the repository lightweight):

```bash
python code/chapter_01/generate_sample_ddr.py
```

This populates `datasets/sample_ddrs/` with five synthetic reports.

## 4. Render the book

```bash
quarto render
```

Output is written to `_book/`. For live-editing a single chapter while
you write:

```bash
quarto preview chapters/chapter_01.qmd
```

## 5. Working in Positron

Positron runs Quarto documents natively — open any `.qmd` file and use
the **Render** button in the editor toolbar, or run individual code
cells interactively (each ` ```{python} ` block behaves like a Jupyter
cell). Recommended setup:

1. Open the `book/` folder as your Positron workspace root.
2. Select the `.venv` interpreter created in Step 2 via the interpreter
   picker (bottom status bar).
3. Use the **Quarto: Preview** command for live reload while editing a
   chapter.
4. Use the **Console** pane to run chapter scripts interactively
   (`python code/chapter_01/read_ddr.py ...`) — this is the fastest way
   to experiment with the code before committing it to a `.qmd`.

## 6. Cloning the companion pipeline (for Part II)

Chapters 6–12 reference [DDR_RAG_Pipeline](https://github.com/djimrastephane/DDR_RAG_Pipeline),
a real, working DDR intelligence platform built and validated against a
171-report drilling campaign. It's a separate repository — clone it
alongside this book's repository, not inside it:

```bash
cd ..                      # up from book/ to your projects root
git clone https://github.com/djimrastephane/DDR_RAG_Pipeline.git
cd DDR_RAG_Pipeline
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Follow its own `README.md` Quickstart to run the full pipeline and
Streamlit dashboard against its sample data, or your own DDR archive.

::: {.callout-important title="This repository is private"}
`DDR_RAG_Pipeline` is currently a private repository. If you're reading
this as part of the author's own workflow, you already have access. If
you're a reader without access, Part II chapters still teach every
technique in full — the simplified code in `code/chapter_06/` through
`code/chapter_12/` in *this* book's repository runs standalone and
requires no access to the companion repository.
:::

## 7. A note on data handling

Daily Drilling Reports are operationally sensitive. Before running any of
this book's code against your own archive:

- Never commit real DDR PDFs, extracted text, or derived artefacts
  (embeddings, indexes) to a public repository. Both `datasets/` (this
  book) and `data/` (the companion repository) are gitignored by default
  for exactly this reason.
- If you're experimenting on a shared machine, confirm your organisation's
  data classification policy covers running third-party Python packages
  (embedding models, OCR engines) against confidential well data.
- The synthetic sample DDRs used throughout Part I are fully fictional —
  safe to commit, share, and publish.

## 8. Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Likely cause |
|---|---|
| `quarto render` fails on a code cell | Activate `.venv` before rendering — Quarto uses whichever Python is on `PATH`. Confirm with `which python` and `python -c "import pdfplumber"`. |
| `ModuleNotFoundError: sentence_transformers` (Chapter 4+) | Re-run `pip install -r requirements.txt`; this is a heavier dependency and sometimes needs a separate `pip install torch` first on some platforms. |
| FAISS import error on Apple Silicon (Chapter 8+) | Ensure you installed `faiss-cpu`, not `faiss-gpu` — the latter has no macOS wheel. |
| PDF generation script produces no output | Confirm you're running it from the `book/` directory, so its relative output path resolves to `datasets/sample_ddrs/`. |
